attractions.name: 16th Street Mall (The Denver Way)
attractions.description: The 16th Street Mall is a mile-long pedestrian-friendly promenade in the heart of downtown Denver, lined with over 300 stores, restaurants, and attractions. Designed by renowned architect I.M. Pei, the mall features a unique granite pattern inspired by a western diamondback rattlesnake and serves as the city's central social hub. Visitors can explore the area on foot or via the free MallRide shuttle bus that runs the length of the street. (Visit Denver, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Individual business hours vary; MallRide shuttle operates 5:30 AM - 12:00 AM weekdays, with slightly shorter weekend hours) (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Denver)
highlights: 1.25-mile pedestrian promenade designed by I.M. Pei (Wikipedia)
Free 16th Street FreeRide shuttle buses (RTD-Denver)
Denver Pavilions shopping and dining complex (Visit Denver)
Historic Daniels & Fisher Tower (Visit Denver)
Unique public art installations like 'Howdy Trouty' and 'Aspen Grove Climber' (Visit Denver)
Vibrant patio culture and people-watching opportunities (Visit Denver)
attractions.tips: Take the free MallRide shuttle to quickly traverse the length of the mall. The area is best for people-watching at one of the many outdoor cafes, especially during the lively afternoon and evening hours. Check out the Denver Pavilions for movies and more shopping. (Visit Denver, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Union Station, Civic Center Park, Colorado State Capitol, Larimer Square, Denver Performing Arts Complex (Visit Denver)

attractions.name: Ball Arena (formerly Pepsi Center)
attractions.description: Ball Arena, formerly known as the Pepsi Center, is a premier multi-purpose indoor sports and entertainment facility located in the heart of downtown Denver. Opened in 1999, it serves as the home for the NBA's Denver Nuggets, the NHL's Colorado Avalanche, and the NLL's Colorado Mammoth. The venue hosts over 250 events annually, ranging from professional sporting matches to world-class concerts and family shows (Ball Arena About, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Public Tour Schedule: Monday, Wednesday, Friday at 10:30 AM and 1:00 PM. No tours on Tuesdays, Thursdays, Saturdays, Sundays, or holidays. Arena event hours vary by schedule (Ball Arena Tours).
attractions.days_open: Open daily for scheduled events; Public tours offered Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays (Ball Arena FAQ, Ball Arena Tours).
attractions.admission: Tour Prices: Adult (13-54): $15, Children (3-12): $12, Senior (55+): $12, Military: $10, Children (0-2): Free. Event ticket prices vary by performance and seating tier (Ball Arena Tours, Ticketmaster).
highlights: Home to the 2023 NBA Champion Denver Nuggets and three-time Stanley Cup Champion Colorado Avalanche (Ball Arena About).
Spans 675,000 square feet across five levels on a massive 45-acre site in the Central Platte Valley (Ball Arena About).
Features the Grand Atrium sculpture, a 2,000-pound bronze work that is 1.5 times life-size (Ball Arena About).
Equipped with a cutting-edge JBL Vertec line array sound system designed for optimal acoustics (Ball Arena About).
Offers exclusive behind-the-scenes tours including access to premium suites and team locker rooms (Ball Arena Tours).
attractions.tips: All tickets must be purchased online as there is no in-person box office for initial purchases. Download the Altitude Presents mobile app before arrival to manage your electronic tickets. Be sure to check the strict bag policy, which typically limits items to small clutches or clear bags, and arrive early for security screening (Ball Arena FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Elitch Gardens Theme Park, Downtown Aquarium, Larimer Square, Denver Union Station, Meow Wolf's Convergence Station (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Ball Arena (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Ball Arena is a premier multi-purpose indoor arena located in the heart of downtown Denver, Colorado. It serves as the home venue for the Denver Nuggets (NBA), the Colorado Avalanche (NHL), and the Colorado Mammoth (NLL). Opened in 1999 and formerly known as the Pepsi Center, it hosts a wide range of concerts, sports, and special events throughout the year (Wikipedia, Ball Arena).
attractions.hours: Arena: Varies by event. Altitude Athletics Team Store: Mon-Sat 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM (closes at 4:00 PM on game days). Venue Tours: Mon, Wed, Fri at 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM; Sat at 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM (Ball Arena, Colorado.com).
attractions.days_open: Daily for scheduled events; Team Store is open Monday through Saturday (Ball Arena).
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically ranges from $20 to $500+ depending on the performance or game (Ticketmaster, Vivid Seats). Tours are approximately $20 for adults (Colorado.com).
highlights: Home to the 2023 NBA Champion Denver Nuggets and three-time Stanley Cup Champion Colorado Avalanche (Ball Arena).
Features a massive 4K center-hung videoboard, one of the largest in professional sports (Wikipedia).
Committed to sustainability, transitioning to 100% recyclable aluminum packaging through a partnership with Ball Corporation (Ball Arena).
Houses a stunning suspended sculpture in the Grand Atrium depicting athletes in action (Wikipedia).
A versatile venue that has hosted the 2008 Democratic National Convention and numerous world-class concert tours (Wikipedia).
Backstage VIP tours offer fans a behind-the-scenes look at locker rooms and exclusive areas (Colorado.com).
attractions.tips: Ball Arena is a cashless venue; only credit/debit cards and mobile payments are accepted. Arrive at least 60-90 minutes before event time to navigate security and bag checks. The arena is easily accessible via the RTD Light Rail at the Ball Arena-Elitch Gardens station (Ball Arena).
attractions.nearby: Elitch Gardens Theme Park, Downtown Aquarium, Larimer Square, Union Station, and Meow Wolf Denver (Tripadvisor, Reddit).

attractions.name: Boettcher Concert Hall
attractions.description: Boettcher Concert Hall is the nation’s first symphony hall in the round, designed to provide an intimate 360-degree acoustic experience where 80% of the seats are within 65 feet of the stage (Denver Performing Arts Complex). It serves as the home of the Colorado Symphony and hosts a variety of performances ranging from classical masterworks to movie-themed concerts and community events (Colorado Symphony).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Saturday, 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m.; Sunday, Closed. Venue doors typically open 60 minutes prior to performances (Colorado Symphony).
attractions.days_open: Monday–Saturday (Box Office); Event days vary (Colorado Symphony)
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance; typical ranges are $20–$131 for standard concerts and $10 for children's tickets (Colorado Symphony). Discounts are often available for students, teachers, and military personnel (Colorado Symphony).
highlights: First symphony hall in the round in the United States, built in 1978 (Wikipedia).
Permanent home to the world-class Colorado Symphony (Colorado Symphony).
Unique 360-degree design ensures intimacy, with most seats very close to the performers (Denver Performing Arts Complex).
Acoustically tuned with canted walls and a 'wave-like' band to disperse sound and prevent echoes (Wikipedia).
Seating capacity of 2,679 with custom-designed steam-bent wood seats (Denver Performing Arts Complex).
Part of the Denver Performing Arts Complex, one of the largest performing arts centers in the U.S. (Oakwell Beer Spa).
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 60 minutes early to navigate security and parking (Oakwell Beer Spa). There is no formal dress code; comfort is encouraged, though business casual is common (CBS Colorado). Use the RTD Light Rail (Theatre District/Convention Center stop) to avoid downtown traffic (Oakwell Beer Spa). Only drinks in plastic cups are allowed in the hall; food must be consumed in the lobby (Colorado Symphony).
attractions.nearby: Temple Hoyne Buell Theater, Ellie Caulkins Opera House, Colorado Convention Center, 16th Street Mall, and Sculpture Park (Trivago).

attractions.name: Colorado Convention Center
attractions.description: The Colorado Convention Center is a massive, multi-purpose facility in downtown Denver known for its state-of-the-art design and "user-friendly" layout. Opened in 1990 and significantly expanded in 2004 and 2024, it hosts over 250 events annually, ranging from professional conferences to major fan expos and concerts. Wikipedia, Official Site
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM (Note: Specific event hours vary; parking is accessible 24/7). Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Admission to the building is generally free for the public to view art like the Big Blue Bear; however, ticket prices for specific events, trade shows, and performances vary significantly by organizer and tier. Vivid Seats, Official Site
highlights: "I See What You Mean" (The Big Blue Bear), a 40-foot iconic public art sculpture peering into the lobby.
Bellco Theatre, a premier 5,000-seat indoor amphitheater used for concerts and keynote speakers.
Over 584,000 square feet of contiguous exhibit space divisible into six individual halls.
The Mile High Ballroom and Four Seasons Ballroom, offering elegant spaces for large-scale galas and meetings.
Integrated RTD light rail station (Theatre District/Convention Center) for seamless city access.
LEED Gold certification, reflecting the center's commitment to industry-leading sustainability practices.
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the facility is vast; utilize the RTD light rail to avoid expensive downtown parking; and make sure to visit the exterior of the building on 14th Street to photograph the famous Big Blue Bear sculpture. Tripadvisor, Official Site
attractions.nearby: Denver Performing Arts Complex, 16th Street Mall, Larimer Square, Ball Arena, Coors Field, and the Downtown Aquarium. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Community College of Denver
attractions.description: Community College of Denver (CCD) is a public community college located on the historic Auraria Campus in downtown Denver. It is unique as the only community college in the nation to share its campus and facilities with two four-year universities: Metropolitan State University of Denver and the University of Colorado Denver (Wikipedia, Community College of Denver).
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 8:00am - 6:00pm, Friday: 8:00am - 5:00pm, Saturday: CLOSED, Sunday: 12:00pm - 4:00pm (Auraria Library)
attractions.admission: Free to visit campus; Tuition for residents with COF is approximately $164.70 per credit hour (Community College of Denver)
highlights: Only community college in the nation to share a campus with two four-year universities (Community College of Denver)
Offers over 100 degree and certificate programs in various pathways (Community College of Denver)
Designated as a Hispanic-Serving Institution (HSI) (Community College of Denver)
Home to the Advanced Manufacturing Center (AMC) (Community College of Denver)
Voted #1 community college by Colorado Sun readers (Community College of Denver)
Located on the 150-acre Auraria Campus in the heart of vibrant downtown Denver (Community College of Denver)
attractions.tips: Use public transportation like the RTD Light Rail, which has two stations on campus. If driving, register your vehicle to avoid premium parking rates for nights and weekends. Visitors can enjoy shared campus services at the Tivoli Student Union or the Auraria Library (Community College of Denver).
attractions.nearby: Larimer Square, Denver Art Museum, Ball Arena (Pepsi Center), Cherry Creek Trail, Civic Center Park (Wikipedia, Visit Denver)

attractions.name: Denver Skatepark (also known as Downtown Denver Skatepark) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Denver Skatepark is a premier 60,000-square-foot facility located in the heart of downtown Denver, Colorado (Wikipedia). As one of the largest free public skateparks in the United States, it offers a vast array of terrain including deep bowls, vertical pipes, and extensive street features (Visit Denver). The park caters to skateboarders, bikers, and bladers of all skill levels, providing a vibrant community space with stunning views of the city skyline (Airial Travel).
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Trucks and Fins, Mile High Mamas)
attractions.admission: Free (Trucks and Fins, Airial Travel)
highlights: Over 60,000 square feet of expansive concrete terrain (Wikipedia)
Diverse mix of deep vertical bowls and vertical pipes (Visit Denver)
Designated street section with rails, ledges, and stairs (Airial Travel)
Integrated snake runs for unique carved transitions (Airial Travel)
On-site night lighting for evening skating sessions (Trucks and Fins)
Stunning views of the Denver city skyline (Airial Travel)
attractions.tips: The concrete surface is exceptionally smooth and can be very slick, so take time to adjust your riding (Reddit). The park operates on a "use at your own risk" policy, and visitors are strongly encouraged to wear full protective gear (Visit Denver). Parking in the surrounding area can fill up quickly on busy days, so arriving early or using public transit is recommended (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Commons Park (DenverGov), Confluence Park (Wikipedia), South Platte River (Wikipedia), RiNo Art District (Eric J. Kuhns), Downtown Denver (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Elitch Gardens Theme & Water Park Wikipedia
attractions.description: Elitch Gardens Theme & Water Park is Colorado’s only world-class combination theme and water park, located in the heart of downtown Denver. It features over 50 attractions, including thrilling roller coasters, family-friendly rides, and a 20-acre water park called Island Kingdom. Elitch Gardens, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 2000 Elitch Circle, Denver, CO 80204 Elitch Gardens
attractions.hours: Typical Season: Mon-Thu 10:30am – 7:00pm or 8:00pm; Fri-Sat 10:30am – 8:00pm or 9:00pm; Sun 10:30am – 7:00pm or 8:00pm. Hours vary by date (e.g., 9:00am - 4:00pm for some school events). Elitch Gardens
attractions.days_open: Daily (late May through early August); Weekends (April, early May, September, October) Elitch Gardens
attractions.admission: Adult/Any Day: $72.99 ($57.99 online); Kids (2 and under): Free; Season Passes: $79.99 - $109.99 (sale prices); Military: 1 complimentary admission during Military Appreciation (May 23–25) Elitch Gardens, Elitch Gardens
highlights: Twister III: Storm Chaser wooden coaster Wikipedia
Island Kingdom Water Park (included with admission) Elitch Gardens
Meow Wolf's Kaleidoscape immersive dark ride Wikipedia
300-foot Tower of Doom freefall ride Wikipedia
Historic 1920 Philadelphia Toboggan Company Carousel Wikipedia
Seasonal events like Fright Fest and Elitch Holidays Elitch Gardens
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online or at local King Soopers for significant discounts. The park is 'Cash-to-Card,' so bring electronic payment or use on-site kiosks. Arrive early to hit popular coasters like Twister III and Boomerang first. TripAdvisor, Elitch Gardens
attractions.nearby: Ball Arena, Empower Field at Mile High, Downtown Aquarium, Children's Museum of Denver Wikipedia, Google Maps

attractions.name: University of Colorado Denver (CU Denver)
attractions.description: The University of Colorado Denver (CU Denver) is the state's premier public urban research university, located in the heart of downtown Denver. Part of the University of Colorado system, it offers more than 100 top-ranked degree programs and serves as a major engine for innovation and research in the Rocky Mountain region (Wikipedia, CU Denver).
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 7:00 am - 6:00 pm (Public Access), 7:00 am - 8:00 pm (Student Access); Saturday - Sunday: 8:00 am - 5:00 pm (Student Access) (CU Denver).
attractions.admission: Free (for visiting campus and public areas); Tuition and fees apply for enrolled students (CU Denver).
highlights: Located on the 127-acre Auraria Campus, which it shares with two other institutions in a unique urban arrangement (Wikipedia).
The Tivoli Student Union is housed in a reclaimed historic brewery that originally closed in 1969 (Wikipedia).
Ranked among R1: Doctoral Universities for very high research activity (Wikipedia).
Features the Student Commons Building, a modern hub for student services and campus life (CU Denver).
Strong partnerships with major Denver businesses and cultural institutions (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: The campus is highly walkable and easily accessible via the RTD Light Rail at the Colfax at Auraria or Auraria West stations. Prospective students should schedule a guided campus tour through the Office of Admissions for a full experience (CU Denver).
attractions.nearby: Larimer Square, Ball Arena, Elitch Gardens, Colorado Convention Center, 16th Street Mall, Denver Union Station (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Center for Colorado Women's History (at Byers-Evans House Museum)
attractions.description: The Center for Colorado Women's History is a historic house museum in Denver that focuses on the stories, memories, and lives of women in Colorado. Located in a beautifully preserved 1883 Italianate home, it offers guided tours that explore the lives of the families who lived there and the broader impact of women on the state's history. History Colorado, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1310 Bannock St., Denver, CO 80204 History Colorado
attractions.region: Golden Triangle / Civic Center Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10 AM - 4 PM; Sunday: 12 PM - 4 PM; Tuesdays in June, July, and August: Open late until 7 PM History Colorado
attractions.admission: Adult (19+): $10, Senior (60+): $8, Children (18 & under): Free, Active Military & Family: Free from Memorial Day to Labor Day History Colorado
highlights: Built in 1883 for Rocky Mountain News founder William Byers and later owned by the Evans family. Wikipedia
Restored to the 1912–1924 period with approximately 90% of the original furnishings. Tripadvisor
Features unique architectural details such as a cast iron widow's walk and Lincrusta-Walton archways. Denver Architecture Foundation
The first state museum dedicated to the past, present, and future of Colorado women. History Colorado
Houses the 'Votes and Dreams' exhibit highlighting Colorado's women activists. History Colorado
attractions.tips: Guided house tours are the primary way to view the interior; street and lot parking are available nearby, with the Civic Center Cultural Complex garage being the most convenient. History Colorado
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Clyfford Still Museum, Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art, Civic Center Park, Colorado State Capitol History Colorado, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Clyfford Still Museum
attractions.description: The Clyfford Still Museum is a single-artist institution dedicated to the life and work of the renegade Abstract Expressionist painter Clyfford Still. Designed specifically to house his massive estate, the museum displays approximately 93% of the artist's lifetime output across nine galleries. Clyfford Still Museum, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1250 Bannock Street, Denver, CO 80204 Clyfford Still Museum
attractions.region: Golden Triangle Creative District Clyfford Still Museum
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday-Thursday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Friday: 12 – 7 p.m.; Saturday-Sunday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m. Clyfford Still Museum
attractions.admission: In-State Adult: $15.00, Out-of-State Adult: $18.00, In-State Senior: $13.00, Out-of-State Senior: $15.00, Student (with ID): $10.00 ($1 onsite for Colorado students), Teacher (with ID): $10.00, Military: Free (Active/Retired + up to 5 family members from May 16-Sep 7), Children (17 and under): Free, Museums for All/Explorer Pass: $1.00 (up to 4 adults with EBT card), Indigenous people: Free (onsite), Same name as Clyfford or Patricia Still: Free (onsite) Clyfford Still Museum, Clyfford Still Museum Discounts
highlights: Nine galleries showcasing Still's 60-year career from early figurative works to monumental late abstractions Clyfford Still Museum
Abstract Expressions: a multisensory sound and garden installation on two outdoor terraces Clyfford Still Museum
A hands-on art studio for visitors of all ages to create their own artwork Clyfford Still Museum
The Allied Works-designed building featuring a unique textured concrete exterior and light-filtering ceilings Allied Works
The artist's personal archives, including journals and letters, offering insight into his enigmatic life Clyfford Still Museum
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance to receive a 50% discount on nearby parking at select surface lots. Most visitors spend about 1-2 hours exploring the collection. Use the free digital guide on the Bloomberg Connects app for deeper insights into the art. Clyfford Still Museum, Bo and Sherpa Travel
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art, History Colorado Center, Denver Public Library (Central Library), Civic Center Park Allied Works, Google Maps

attractions.name: Counterterrorism Education Learning Lab (CELL)
attractions.description: The Counterterrorism Education Learning Lab (CELL) is a non-profit, nonpartisan organization dedicated to preventing terrorism through education, empowerment, and engagement. Its state-of-the-art exhibit and training initiatives increase public awareness and preparedness to promote public safety and security (CELL About).
attractions.region: Golden Triangle Creative District / Capitol Hill (Visit Denver Neighborhoods)
attractions.hours: Group Tours: Tue 10 AM – 5 PM, Thu 1 PM – 5 PM; General Admission: Fri 10 AM – 4 PM (CELL Plan Your Visit)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Thursday (Group Tours); Friday (General Admission) (CELL Plan Your Visit)
attractions.admission: Adults: $16, Seniors (65+): $12, Government/Military (with ID): $12, Students (with ID): $12, Group Tours (10+ people): $10 per person, SCFD Free Days: Free (CELL Tickets)
highlights: Vitriol & Violence: A powerful multimedia experience addressing the rise of dangerous views (CELL Exhibit).
Countering Today's Threats: Interactive simulations where visitors learn to identify suspicious behaviors (CELL Exhibit).
Civil Liberties & National Security: Exploration of the balance between safety, hate speech, and privacy rights (CELL Exhibit).
Rooted in Extremism: A look into the ideologies of various extremist movements (CELL Exhibit).
World Trade Center Artifact: A moving exhibit piece from the September 11 attacks (Tripadvisor).
attractions.tips: The exhibit is recommended for visitors ages 14 and older due to the complexity and graphic nature of the content. Tickets use a timed entry system and should be purchased in advance (CELL Plan Your Visit).
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Clyfford Still Museum, Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art, History Colorado Center, United States Mint at Denver (Visit Denver Neighborhoods)

attractions.name: Denver Art Museum
attractions.description: The Denver Art Museum is one of the largest art museums between Chicago and the West Coast, housing a collection of more than 70,000 works. It is renowned for its extensive American Indian art collection and its stunning architectural campus, which includes the iconic Gio Ponti-designed Martin Building and Daniel Libeskind's Hamilton Building. Denver Art Museum, CityPass
attractions.hours: Sunday: 10 am–5 pm; Monday: 10 am–5 pm; Tuesday: 10 am–8 pm; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 10 am–5 pm; Friday: 10 am–5 pm; Saturday: 10 am–5 pm. Denver Art Museum
attractions.admission: Adults: $22–25 (CO Residents), $27–30 (Non-Residents); Seniors (65+): $19–22 (CO Residents), $24–27 (Non-Residents); Students, Teachers, Military: $19–22 (CO Residents), $24–27 (Non-Residents); Youth (18 & under): Free; Members: Free. SNAP cardholders: $1-$5. Denver Art Museum
highlights: Extensive American Indian Art collection representing virtually every tribe across the continent.
Modern and Contemporary Art collection with over 12,000 pieces, including works by Warhol and Picasso.
World-renowned Western American art collection featuring masters like Frederic Remington and Charles M. Russell.
Architectural marvels: the fortress-like Martin Building and the sharp-angled Hamilton Building.
Textile Art & Fashion collection featuring over 5,000 pieces, including ancient Chinese robes and American quilts.
Sie Welcome Center and interactive learning spaces for all ages.
attractions.tips: Plan to spend at least half a day (2-3 hours minimum) to experience the 14 unique collections. Admission for youth 18 and under is always free. Check the online calendar for monthly free admission days sponsored by SCFD. Use the free Bloomberg Connects app for a digital guide through the galleries. Denver Art Museum, CityPass
attractions.nearby: Clyfford Still Museum, Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art, Colorado Convention Center (Blue Bear), Colorado State Capitol, Civic Center Park. Visit Denver

attractions.name: Denver Central Library (Denver Public Library)
attractions.description: The Denver Central Library is the flagship of the Denver Public Library system and a prominent architectural landmark in the Golden Triangle neighborhood. Designed by renowned architect Michael Graves, the building features an iconic postmodern design and recently underwent extensive renovations to modernize its public spaces. The library serves as a major cultural hub, offering vast collections, makerspaces, and specialized research services for the community. (Denver Public Library, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Fri: 10 a.m. - 5 p.m., Sat-Sun: 11 a.m. - 5 p.m. (Denver Public Library)
attractions.admission: Free (Denver Public Library)
highlights: Award-winning postmodern architecture designed by Michael Graves. (Wikipedia)
Extensive Western History and Genealogy collections and archives. (Tripadvisor)
Innovative ideaLAB makerspace with tools for creative projects. (Denver Public Library)
State-of-the-art Children's and Teen Libraries following recent renovations. (Denver Public Library)
Park View event space for author visits and community programming. (Denver Public Library)
Specialized research appointments for business, patents, and trademarks. (Remitly)
attractions.tips: Use your library card to check out free museum passes for nearby attractions like the Denver Art Museum. Be sure to notice the unique fossil-embedded flooring in Schlessman Hall. Visitors should be aware of the area's high concentration of people experiencing homelessness. (Denver Public Library, Tripadvisor, Remitly)
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Civic Center Park, Colorado State Capitol, History Colorado Center, Clyfford Still Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Goodwin Fine Art
attractions.description: Goodwin Fine Art represents regionally and nationally recognized contemporary artists working across various media, including painting, photography, and sculpture (Meer). The gallery features rotating exhibitions every six to eight weeks, focusing on emerging and established artists within a modern, industrial-style space (303 Magazine, Art & Antiques Magazine).
attractions.address: 1255 Delaware Street, Denver, CO 80204, United States
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Friday: 11:00 AM – 5:30 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Closed Sunday and Monday (Meer)
attractions.admission: Free (Stockton Art League, Denver Art Museum)
highlights: Features contemporary works in painting, photography, drawing, sculpture, and printmaking (Meer)
Located in the heart of Denver's Golden Triangle Museum District (CaFE)
Hosts rotating solo and group exhibitions every 6-8 weeks (5280)
Owned and curated by Tina Goodwin, an expert with over 30 years of experience in the art world (303 Magazine)
Specializes in contemporary emerging, mid-career, and established artists (CaFE)
Participates in the district's lively First Friday art events (Denver Art Dealers Association)
attractions.tips: Visit during First Friday receptions for a chance to meet artists and enjoy a lively gallery atmosphere (CaFE). Its location makes it a perfect stop as part of a larger tour of the Golden Triangle's many museums and galleries (5280).
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Clyfford Still Museum, Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art, Walker Fine Art, William Havu Gallery (CaFE, Denver Art Dealers Association)

attractions.name: History Colorado Center
attractions.description: The History Colorado Center is a dynamic cultural hub in Denver's Golden Triangle neighborhood, serving as a gateway to the multifaceted history of the Centennial State. Opened in 2012, this Smithsonian Affiliate features four floors of interactive exhibits, high-tech storytelling, and immersive experiences designed for visitors of all ages. The center blends historical artifacts with digital innovation to explore Colorado's diverse narratives from ancient cultures to the present day (History Colorado, Visit Denver).
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day; Early Closing (3:00 PM) on Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve (History Colorado)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Except Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day)
attractions.admission: Adults (19+): $20; Children (18 & under): Free; History Colorado Members: Free; Active Duty Military & Families (Memorial Day–Labor Day): Free; Veterans: $2 off general admission (Free on Veterans Day); SNAP/EBT Cardholders: $1 per person (up to 10 people); Adult Groups (10+): $18 per person (History Colorado)
highlights: Living West: An environmental history exhibit exploring Mesa Verde, the Dust Bowl, and the Rocky Mountains.
Colorado Stories: Interactive displays featuring a 1915 wooden ski jump simulator and an immersive silver mine.
Destination Colorado: A journey back in time to the 1920s town of Keota on the high plains.
Time Machine: A massive 40-by-60-foot floor map of Colorado that visitors can explore using "steam punk" machines to reveal historical stories.
Zoom In: The Centennial State in 100 Objects: Highlights the state's history through 100 iconic and significant artifacts.
The Sand Creek Massacre: The Betrayal that Changed Cheyenne and Arapaho People Forever: A powerful and educational exhibit on a pivotal historical event.
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2 to 2.5 hours to comfortably explore all four floors of exhibits. Afternoons are generally quieter than mornings for a more relaxed visit. Don't miss the Stephen H. Hart Research Center on the second floor for in-depth state records, and consider dining at Café Rendezvous for local flavors (CityPASS, History Colorado).
attractions.nearby: Denver Art Museum, Clyfford Still Museum, Colorado State Capitol, Center for Colorado Women's History, Denver Public Library (Central Branch) (Colorado.com)

attractions.name: McNichols Civic Center Building
attractions.description: The McNichols Civic Center Building is a historic Greek Revival landmark that serves as a contemporary hub for arts, culture, and events. Originally built in 1909 as Denver's first Carnegie Library, it was restored and reopened in 2012 to host exhibitions, performances, and private gatherings. mcnicholsbuilding.com, Denver Arts & Venues

attractions.name: The Kirkland (Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art)
attractions.description: The Kirkland (formerly the Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Art) is a celebrated institution that merged with the Denver Art Museum in October 2024. It brings to life 150 years of artistic innovation through an immersive journey of international decorative art, Colorado’s rich artistic heritage, and the visionary work of modern painter Vance Kirkland. The collection is housed in a striking 38,500-square-foot building designed by Jim Olson, which incorporates Kirkland's historic 1911 studio. Denver Art Museum
attractions.hours: Sun, Mon, Thu, Fri, Sat: 10 am–5 pm; Tue: 10 am–8 pm; Wed: Closed. Denver Art Museum

Members: Free. Note: Peak pricing applies on weekends and holidays. Denver Art Museum
highlights: International Decorative Arts spanning from Arts & Crafts to Postmodern design.
Colorado Fine Art collection showcasing the state's talent from the 1840s to the present.
Vance Kirkland Retrospective featuring the work of Colorado's most distinguished modern painter.
The Vance Kirkland Studio, a historic member of Historic Artists’ Homes and Studios.
Salon-style display where fine and decorative art are exhibited together in domestic-like vignettes.
Distinctive 'golden' building architecture designed by Jim Olson of Olson Kundig. Denver Art Museum

Visitors can explore the facility's Gothic-Renaissance architecture and witness the automated process of turning metal blanks into shiny currency while learning about the Mint's pivotal role in Western American history. attractions.name: Confluence Park
attractions.description: Confluence Park is a scenic urban park located at the meeting point of Cherry Creek and the South Platte River in Denver's Lower Downtown (LoDo). Established in 1974, it marks the historic site where gold was discovered in 1858, leading to the founding of Denver. The park features a whitewater kayak run, sandy river banks for sunbathing, and serves as a central hub for the city's extensive bike trail network. Wikipedia, Visit Denver
attractions.hours: Daily 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM Apple Maps
attractions.admission: Free (Park access); Denver Trolley: $7 Adults (13+), $3 Children (4-12), Free (under 4) Denver Trolley

Sandy beach areas for sunbathing and river access Uncover Colorado
Historic Denver Trolley departures to the Downtown Aquarium and Children's Museum Denver Trolley
Shoemaker Plaza, a multi-level gathering space with views of the river and skyline TCLF
Extensive trail network connecting the Cherry Creek and South Platte River trails Wikipedia
Scenic panoramic views of downtown Denver and the Millennium Bridge Visit Denver
attractions.tips: The park is a popular spot for kayaking and tubing, but visitors should check water conditions before entering. It is a major bike trail junction, so be mindful of cyclists when walking. Parking can be found in the surrounding LoDo neighborhood or near the REI store. The Denver Trolley is a great way to visit nearby attractions like the Aquarium with kids. Uncover Colorado, Denver Trolley

attractions.name: Coors Field
attractions.description: Coors Field is a premier Major League Baseball stadium located in downtown Denver, serving as the home of the Colorado Rockies. Opened in 1995, the ballpark is renowned for its high elevation and hitter-friendly environment, featuring a unique "row of purple seats" exactly one mile above sea level. The stadium offers a classic baseball experience with modern amenities, including the Rooftop bar and the SandLot Brewery. Wikipedia, MLB.com

Interactive 'Water World' fountain in center field that shoots water after home runs
attractions.tips: Arrive early to enjoy The Rooftop and the interactive areas; note that Coors Field is a cashless venue. Be prepared for high-altitude effects and variable weather. Purchase tour tickets in advance as they are limited. MLB.com

attractions.name: Green Russell
attractions.description: Green Russell was a premier chef-driven cocktail joint located beneath Denver's historic Larimer Square, pioneering the city's modern speakeasy scene. The subterranean bar was renowned for its sophisticated atmosphere, meticulously handcrafted cocktails featuring house-made bitters, and a clandestine entrance hidden behind a pie shop. It offered an intimate, old-world experience characterized by plush seating and a commitment to the art of mixology.

The venue features "Beer Downstairs" for social gatherings and "Beats Upstairs" in the Electric Loft, where resident DJs spin Top 40, EDM, and hip-hop. It is particularly known as a hub for Philadelphia sports fans and offers a menu of classic bar fare like smashburgers and Philly cheesesteaks. (Official Website)

attractions.description: The Museum of Contemporary Art Denver (MCA Denver) is an innovative forum for contemporary art that inspires and challenges audiences through rotating exhibitions and public programs. Housed in a cutting-edge LEED-certified building designed by David Adjaye, the museum features five distinct galleries showcasing regional, national, and international artists. Its vibrant atmosphere is complemented by a rooftop café and bar offering panoramic views of the city skyline (Official Website, Visit Denver).

The establishment boasts a high-energy dining room with a copper-topped bar, an exhibition kitchen, and simple decor designed to let the cuisine shine. Led by James Beard Award-winning Chef Jennifer Jasinski and owner Beth Gruitch, it is perennially ranked as one of Denver's top culinary destinations. (Rioja Official Site)

attractions.description: Founded in 1994, Great Divide Brewing Company is one of Denver's most celebrated craft breweries, known for brewing balanced yet assertive beers like the iconic Yeti Imperial Stout. The RiNo location, a Brewery & Roadhouse, offers a full line-up of award-winning ales alongside elevated comfort food in a vibrant, family-friendly setting.
attractions.region: RiNo (River North Art District) / Five Points
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Award-winning craft beers including 19 Great American Beer Festival medals
Full kitchen serving elevated comfort food like the All American Burger and Hazy IPA Fish & Chips
Rotating seasonal and small-batch beer series including the Yeti Imperial Stout varieties
Spacious indoor seating and outdoor patio in the heart of the RiNo Art District
Family-friendly atmosphere with a dedicated kids' menu and stroller-friendly space
Full bar featuring innovative cocktails, mocktails, and Stem Ciders options
attractions.tips: Visit during the weekend for their popular daily brunch menu served until 3 PM. If you're looking for limited-release beers, ask about their experimental small-batch series only available at Roadhouse locations.
attractions.nearby: Denver Union Station, Coors Field, RiNo Art District, Backyard on Blake, Larimer Square

attractions.name: Odell Brewing Five Points Brewhouse
attractions.description: Odell Brewing Five Points Brewhouse is a 10-barrel pilot brewery and taproom located in a renovated 1917 historic building. It features two bars with 16 taps, a street-level dog-friendly patio, and a partially-covered rooftop patio, specializing in experimental, location-exclusive small-batch beers Odell Brewing Co, 5280.

Historical 1917 brick building architecture Odell Brewing Co
Regular community events like Walking Club, Run Club, and Trivia Odell Brewing Co

attractions.name: Rule Gallery
attractions.description: Founded in 1991, Rule Gallery champions investigative practices by emerging and mid-career contemporary artists and estates. The gallery operates in both Denver, CO, and Marfa, TX, presenting dynamic exhibitions within its galleries and in unexpected settings. As of 2025, its Denver program features a nomadic satellite model that activates intentional sites throughout the region alongside a showroom in the RiNo district (Rule Gallery, Southwest Contemporary).
attractions.region: RiNo (River North Art District) (RiNo Art District)
attractions.hours: Wednesday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM; also available by appointment (Rule Gallery, RiNo Art District)
attractions.admission: Free (Rule Gallery, Visit Denver)
highlights: Champions investigative practices by emerging and mid-career contemporary artists and artist estates (Rule Gallery).
Established presence in the Denver art scene with over three decades of history since 1991 (Rule Gallery).
Employs an innovative satellite exhibition model to present site-specific, time-based art experiences (Denver Westword).
Maintains an art lounge-style showroom within the Fabrica building in Denver's RiNo Art District (RiNo Art District).
Features a sister gallery in the renowned art destination of Marfa, Texas (Rule Gallery).
Hosted the original "Wish You Were Her/e" neon sign now located at the Ramble Hotel (The Denver Ear).
attractions.tips: If the front reception desk is unattended, visitors should call or text the gallery number to be buzzed in. Free visitor parking is available on three sides of the building nearest Brighton Boulevard, with additional paid garage parking at the rear (RiNo Art District).
attractions.nearby: Alto Gallery, Plinth Gallery, MAE'S, PlatteForum, Globeville Riverfront Art Center (GRACe), and various RiNo Art District murals (RiNo Art District)

attractions.name: The Denver Central Market Official Website
attractions.category: Food Hall & Gourmet Marketplace Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Denver Central Market is a gourmet marketplace and food hall located in a restored 14,000 square-foot 1920s industrial building in the RiNo district. It features a curated selection of 11 local vendors including a bakery, butcher, fish market, and craft cocktail bar, serving as both a dining destination and a specialty grocery store. Official Website, Tabski
attractions.region: RiNo Art District (River North) Official Website
attractions.hours: Food Vendors: Sun–Thu 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri–Sat 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Curio Bar: Mon–Wed 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thu 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free TouriSpo
highlights: 11 artisan food vendors including Izzio Bakery and High Point Creamery Official Website
Housed in the historic H.H. Tammen building from 1928 Tabski
Specialty grocery shopping with fresh seafood, local meats, and produce Joe Coffee, TouriSpo
Curio Bar offering craft cocktails, wine, and local draft beers Official Website, TouriSpo
Vibrant "artsy-industrial" atmosphere in the heart of the RiNo Art District Official Website, Tabski
Indoor and outdoor patio seating Official Website
attractions.tips: Vendor hours may vary from general market hours. Pets are permitted on the outdoor patio but not inside. Public parking is available in the lot directly south of the building or at 27th and Blake for a flat fee. Official Website
attractions.nearby: RiNo Art District galleries and murals, The Source Hotel & Market Hall, Ratio Beerworks, Odell Brewing Co Five Points Brewhouse Visit Denver

attractions.name: Cheesman Park
attractions.description: Cheesman Park is a historic 80-acre urban park and neighborhood in central Denver, known for its expansive lawns and neoclassical pavilion. Once a 19th-century cemetery, it was transformed into a park in 1907 and now serves as a vibrant community gathering spot and a tranquil escape from the city. (Wikipedia, denvergov.org)
attractions.hours: Daily: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (denvergov.org)
attractions.admission: Free (denvergov.org)
highlights: Neoclassical Cheesman Memorial Pavilion built from Yule marble (Wikipedia)
Adjacent to the renowned Denver Botanic Gardens (Wikipedia)
Home to over 1,880 trees from 57 different species (Wikipedia)
Central hub for LGBT events, including the annual PrideFest parade (Wikipedia)
Extensive walking and jogging trails with skyline views (denvergov.org)
Historical significance as a former cemetery, often associated with local ghost stories (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: There is no public parking available within the park; visitors should look for street parking in the surrounding neighborhoods. The park is a popular spot for picnics and sunset viewing at the pavilion. Be mindful of the park's history as a former cemetery, which adds a unique layer to its atmosphere. (denverlocaltours.com, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Denver Botanic Gardens, Molly Brown House Museum, Colorado State Capitol, Denver Art Museum, City Park (Wikipedia, Expedia)

attractions.name: Colorado State Capitol
attractions.description: The Colorado State Capitol is a historic government building known for its gold-leafed dome and its location exactly one mile above sea level. It serves as the home of the Colorado General Assembly and the offices of the Governor, and it is a prominent feature of Denver's Civic Center area. (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 7:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Closed Saturday and Sunday (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Site)
highlights: Gold-leafed dome commemorating the Colorado Gold Rush (Wikipedia)
'Mile High' step located exactly 5,280 feet above sea level (Visit Denver)
Rare Colorado Rose Onyx used throughout the interior (Tripadvisor)
Panoramic views of the Rocky Mountains from the dome observation area (Official Site)
Mr. Brown's Attic museum detailing Colorado's history (Official Site)
attractions.tips: Take the free guided tour to access the dome observation area and outdoor balcony; self-guided tours are allowed but do not include dome access. Arrive early as guided tours are limited to 30 people on a first-come, first-served basis. (Official Site)
attractions.nearby: Civic Center Park, Denver Art Museum, Molly Brown House Museum, United States Mint at Denver (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Molly Brown House Museum (House of Lions)
attractions.description: The Molly Brown House Museum is a beautifully restored 1889 Victorian mansion that served as the home of Margaret “Molly” Brown, the famed socialite, activist, and Titanic survivor. Operated by Historic Denver, Inc., the museum offers guided and self-guided tours that explore her life of philanthropy, political activism, and heroism. Molly Brown House Museum
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Wednesday: 10am – 5pm; Thursday: 10am – 7pm; Friday – Saturday: 10am – 5pm; Sunday: 12pm – 4pm. Last entry is one hour before closing. Molly Brown House Museum
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (Closed Mondays) Molly Brown House Museum
attractions.admission: Guided Tours: Non-Resident Adults $22.04, Juniors (6-18) $15.43, Seniors (70+) $19.84, Military/Teachers/Students $19.84; CO Resident Adults $19.84, Juniors $13.23, Seniors $17.63, Military/Teachers/Students $17.63; Children (5 and under) Free. Self-guided tours start from $15.43. Free admission for SCFD residents on the first Thursday of each month (4–7 p.m.) and select days (March 8, August 26). Molly Brown House Museum
highlights: Restored 1889 Victorian mansion featuring original furniture and artifacts from the Brown family Molly Brown House Museum
Exhibits detailing Margaret Brown’s experience and heroism during the RMS Titanic disaster Molly Brown House Museum
Insights into her political activism, including her 1914 run for the U.S. Senate Molly Brown House Museum
Interactive digital tours available through the Bloomberg Connects App Molly Brown House Museum
Seasonal events such as 'Victorian Horrors' and traditional Victorian teas Yelp
attractions.tips: Tickets should be pre-purchased online as tours often sell out. Large bags, backpacks, and strollers are not permitted inside the historic home. The museum is not air-conditioned and can be warm in the summer, so bringing water is recommended. Molly Brown House Museum
attractions.nearby: Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception, Colorado State Capitol, History Colorado Center, Denver Art Museum, St. John's Cathedral. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: City Park
attractions.description: City Park is Denver's largest and premier regional park, spanning approximately 330 acres in the east-central part of the city. Established in 1882, it serves as a major cultural hub, housing the Denver Zoo and the Denver Museum of Nature & Science amidst expansive green spaces and scenic lakes (Visit Denver, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Tuesday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Wednesday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Thursday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Friday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Saturday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m.; Sunday: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m. (Denver Parks & Recreation)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Denver Parks & Recreation)
attractions.admission: Free (Park entry). Some attractions within the park like the Denver Zoo and Denver Museum of Nature & Science require paid tickets: Adults $25-$27, Children $19-$21 (CityPASS, DMNS).
highlights: Denver Zoo (Visit Denver)
Denver Museum of Nature & Science (Visit Denver)
Ferril Lake and Duck Lake (Wikipedia)
City Park Pavilion and Thatcher Fountain (DenverGov)
One-mile high hiking and jogging trail (Visit Denver)
Summer Sunday night jazz concert series (City Park Jazz)
attractions.tips: For the best views of the Denver skyline and mountains, head to the western shore of Ferril Lake or the steps of the Museum of Nature & Science. Rent a swan pedal boat from Wheel Fun Rentals for a unique perspective of the park from the water (Reddit, Expedia).
attractions.nearby: Bluebird Theater, Denver Botanic Gardens, Colfax Avenue restaurants and retail, City Park Golf Course (Expedia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Denver Museum of Nature & Science (Official Site)
attractions.description: The Denver Museum of Nature & Science is the Rocky Mountain region’s leading resource for informal science education. A variety of exhibitions, programs, and activities help visitors experience the natural wonders of Colorado, Earth, and the universe (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 2001 Colorado Blvd, Denver, CO 80205-5798 (Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Most Fridays: 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Daily (363 days a year; closed Dec. 25 and Aug. 25) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adult (19-64): $25.95; Youth (3-18): $20.95; Senior (65+): $22.95; Children (0-2): Free; Members: Free. SNAP EBT card holders: $3 per person (up to 10 people). Licensed Colorado K-12 educators: Free (Official Site)
highlights: Prehistoric Journey: Chronicles the evolution of life on Earth with dinosaur skeletons like T. rex and Stegosaurus (Official Site, Wikipedia)
Space Odyssey: An immersive exhibit exploring the universe with interactive displays and a full-scale Mars rover replica (Official Site, Wikipedia)
Wildlife Halls: Over 90 animal dioramas showcasing scenes of daily life from various ecosystems across the globe (Official Site, Wikipedia)
Coors Gems & Minerals: Features a re-created mine and the world's largest rhodochrosite crystal, the 'Alma King' (Official Site, Wikipedia)
Gates Planetarium and Infinity Theater: State-of-the-art facilities for cosmic shows and giant-screen film experiences (Official Site, Visit Denver)
Discovery Zone: A dedicated play area for children aged 0-8 to explore paleontology and water physics (Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets in advance online to secure your preferred entry time. Visit the fourth-floor terrace for stunning views of City Park and the Denver skyline. Arrive early on weekends as parking fills up quickly (Official Site, Reddit, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Denver Zoo, City Park, Denver Botanic Gardens, Molly Brown House Museum, Denver Art Museum (Tripadvisor, Visit Denver)

attractions.name: Denver Zoo Conservation Alliance (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Denver Zoo Conservation Alliance is an 80-acre nonprofit zoological garden and conservation organization located in Denver's City Park. Founded in 1896, it is home to over 3,000 animals across more than 500 species and is renowned for its pioneering naturalistic enclosures (Wikipedia) (City Park Alliance).
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.; Member Early Entry: 8:00 a.m. – 9:00 a.m. (Memorial Day through Labor Day) (Official Website) (Official Website).
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults (16-64): $27, Seniors (65+): $24, Youth (3-15): $19, Youth (2 and under): Free. SNAP/EBT cardholders: $3 for up to 10 guests. Seven Community Free Days are offered annually via an online lottery (Official Website) (Official Website).
highlights: Toyota Elephant Passage: A 10-acre habitat that is one of the largest bull elephant environments in the world (Wikipedia).
Predator Ridge: An African savanna exhibit featuring rotating groups of lions, hyenas, and African wild dogs (Official Website).
Primate Panorama: A seven-acre lushly forested area where visitors can see gorillas, orangutans, and various monkeys (Wikipedia).
Tropical Discovery: An indoor tropical paradise housing a public aquarium, herpetarium, and over 250 plant species (Official Website).
Bear Mountain: A historic exhibit on the National Register of Historic Places, noted as the first in the U.S. to use naturalistic enclosures (Wikipedia).
Down Under: An immersive Australian exhibit featuring walk-through yards for kangaroos and wallabies (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Timed-entry ticket reservations must be made online in advance. Parking is free in the adjacent lots and parking garage. Outside food is permitted, making it a great spot for a picnic in the park (Official Website) (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Denver Museum of Nature & Science, City Park, City Park Golf Course, Denver Botanic Gardens (Tripadvisor) (City Park Alliance).

attractions.name: Tattered Cover Book Store
attractions.description: Established in 1971, Tattered Cover Book Store is a legendary Denver institution and one of America's premier independent bookstores. The flagship Colfax location is housed in the historic Bonfils-Lowenstein Theater, offering a multi-level browsing experience with over half a million books in inventory across its locations. Tattered Cover, Denver Architecture Foundation

attractions.hours: Store: Mon-Fri 10:00am-8:00pm, Sat 9:00am-8:00pm, Sun 10:00am-6:00pm; Cafe: Mon-Fri 10:00am-8:00pm, Sat 9:00am-8:00pm, Sun 10:00am-6:00pm Tattered Cover

It features more than 260 businesses, including a curated mix of international brands, local boutiques, and upscale galleries. Cherry Creek North, Visit Denver

A mix of international luxury brands and local boutique shops Cherry Creek North

Chef-driven restaurants and vibrant rooftop bars Cherry Creek North
Signature annual events like the Cherry Creek Arts Festival and Winter Wanderland Cherry Creek North, Wikipedia
Extensive wellness options with over 30 spas and fitness centers Cherry Creek North

attractions.name: Washington Park
attractions.description: Washington Park is a premier 165-acre urban park in Denver, renowned for its picturesque lakes, expansive flower gardens, and historic architecture. Consistently ranked as a local favorite, it offers a vibrant community atmosphere with a 2.6-mile loop for jogging and cycling, tennis courts, and a recreation center. The park's design, influenced by German landscape architect Reinhard Schuetze, includes a replica of Martha Washington’s garden at Mt. Vernon and the iconic 1913 Boathouse (Wikipedia, Visit Denver).
attractions.address: S. Downing St. & E. Louisiana Ave. (Main entry point) and 701 S. Franklin St. (Recreation Center)
attractions.hours: 5:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m. Daily (Denver Parks & Recreation)
attractions.admission: Free (Uncover Colorado)
highlights: Two scenic lakes: Smith Lake and Grasmere Lake, offering paddle boat rentals (Visit Denver)
Extensive flower gardens, including the largest perennial garden in Denver and a replica of Mt. Vernon's garden (Wikipedia)
A 2.6-mile paved perimeter loop popular for jogging, cycling, and skating (Uncover Colorado)
The historic 1913 Washington Park Boathouse Pavilion, a popular venue for events (Denver Gov)
The Washington Park Recreation Center, featuring an indoor pool and fitness facilities (Denver Gov)
attractions.tips: Parking can be very difficult on sunny weekends; arrive early or use public transit like the RTD Light Rail (Louisiana/Pearl station). Dogs are welcome but must be kept on a leash at all times (Visit Denver, Denver Gov).
attractions.nearby: Old South Gaylord Street (boutiques and dining), Cherry Creek Shopping Center, University of Denver, and the historic South High School (WPENA)

attractions.name: Black American West Museum & Heritage Center
attractions.description: The Black American West Museum & Heritage Center is dedicated to documenting and preserving the history of African American pioneers who helped settle and develop the American West. Housed in the historic former residence of Dr. Justina Ford, the first Black female physician in Colorado, the museum features extensive collections of artifacts, photographs, and oral histories of Black cowboys, miners, soldiers, and homesteaders. Official Website, Visit Denver
attractions.hours: Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Closed Sunday–Thursday) Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults: $17, Seniors (65+): $15, Military: $12, Youth/Student (6-17 or with College ID): $12, Children (5 & under): Free. (Prices include fees; walk-ins subject to availability) Official Website
highlights: Housed in the historic 19th-century home of Dr. Justina Ford, Colorado's first Black female physician
Extensive collection of artifacts and photographs documenting Black cowboys and ranchers
Exhibits detailing the lives of Black miners, soldiers (Buffalo Soldiers), and lawmen on the frontier
Information and artifacts from Dearfield, a historic Black agricultural colony in Colorado
Digital museum guide available via the Bloomberg Connects app for an enhanced experience
A National Register of Historic Places landmark structure
attractions.tips: Plan for a visit of at least 1 hour; walk-ins are possible but booking tickets online in advance is recommended as the museum has limited hours. Photography is allowed, but video recordings are prohibited. Check out the rotating digital exhibitions on the Bloomberg Connects app during your visit. Official Website
attractions.nearby: 30th and Downing Station (Light Rail), Blair-Caldwell African American Research Library, Stiles African American Heritage Center, RiNo Art District, Welton Street Cafe, Spangalang Brewery. Tripadvisor, Visit Denver

attractions.name: Five Points Historic Cultural District
attractions.description: Five Points Historic Cultural District, once known as the 'Harlem of the West,' is one of Denver's oldest and most culturally significant neighborhoods. Centered along the Welton Street corridor, it served as a major jazz hub and a core gathering place for Denver's African American community, now featuring a vibrant mix of historic landmarks, soul food institutions, and modern craft breweries.
attractions.address: 2444 Washington St (District Office: 1515 Arapahoe St, Tower 3, Suite 100)
attractions.hours: District Area: Open 24/7 (Public Spaces); District Office: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Monday–Friday)
attractions.days_open: Open daily (public spaces); District Office: Monday–Friday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Historic Welton Street Corridor, the core commercial and cultural hub of the district.
Legacy Jazz Venues like the Rossonian Hotel, which hosted legends such as Billie Holiday and Louis Armstrong.
Blair-Caldwell African American Research Library, documenting the history of African Americans in the West.
Black American West Museum, located in the former home of Dr. Justina Ford.
Annual Juneteenth Music Festival, one of the city's largest cultural celebrations.
Vibrant Street Art and Murals throughout the neighborhood, including the nearby RiNo Art District.
attractions.tips: Take the self-guided Welton Street Cultural District Walking Tour to see historic markers; use the Denver Story Trek cell phone tour for hidden history; visit on a Saturday between mid-June and October for the Mo’Betta Greens Marketplace.
attractions.nearby: Coors Field, RiNo Art District, Downtown Denver, Curtis Park, Blair-Caldwell African American Research Library.

attractions.description: RiNo Art District is a nonprofit arts organization and Certified Colorado Creative District dedicated to supporting artists, creative businesses, and the community. Founded in 2005, it has transformed a formerly industrial area into a vibrant urban hub featuring galleries, studios, murals, and a wide array of creative spaces. RiNo Art District About
attractions.region: Five Points (also encompasses Globeville, Elyria-Swansea, and Cole)

attractions.admission: Free for general district access and most galleries; some special events or guided tours may have fees. RiNo Art District, Delicious Denver Food Tours
highlights: Over 50 art galleries and studios Visit Denver
Extensive collection of murals and street art RiNo Art District
RiNo ArtPark, a community-focused multidisciplinary destination RiNo Art District

A-Line RTD station at 38th & Blake for easy access RiNo Art District
attractions.tips: The best time to visit is during the First Friday Art Walk (1st Friday of each month, 6-9 PM) when many studios open to the public. The district is highly walkable/bikeable with two-way bike lanes on Blake and Larimer. Use the RTD A-Line for convenient transit. RiNo Art District, Visit Denver
attractions.nearby: The Source Hotel + Market Hall, Coors Field, Number Thirty Eight, Denver Public Library (RiNo branch) Delicious Denver Food Tours, The Source Hotel

attractions.name: Snooze, an A.M. Eatery (Ballpark)
attractions.description: Snooze started in Denver in 2006 with a mission to revolutionize the breakfast experience by turning classics upside down. Known for its vibrant mid-century modern atmosphere, it serves as a neighborhood hub for connection over creatively executed, consciously sourced dishes.
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 6:30 AM - 2:30 PM, Saturday - Sunday: 6:30 AM - 3:30 PM
attractions.admission: Free admission (paid dining establishment)
highlights: Signature Pancake Flights with seasonal flavors
Creative twists on Eggs Benedict like the Bella! Bella! Benny
Full bar featuring local craft beers, mimosas, and Bloody Marys
Consciously sourced ingredients including cage-free eggs and responsibly raised meats
Decadent French Toast and breakfast burritos
Vibrant and energetic atmosphere in the historic Ballpark district
attractions.tips: Expect long wait times on weekends; it is highly recommended to join the waitlist via the Snooze app or Yelp before arrival. Arriving early on weekdays or right at opening is a great way to avoid the crowd. Don't miss the 'Pancake Flight' to sample multiple unique flavors in one sitting. (Yelp, Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: Root Down is a popular Denver dining destination housed in a converted 1950s gas station, known for its funky mid-century modern aesthetic and sustainable, globally-inspired cuisine. The restaurant emphasizes organic, natural, and locally-sourced ingredients, including produce from its own backyard gardens, and is 100% employee-owned. Edible Beats Official Website, Shike Design
